description: Confounder: The model assumes any time t plugged into min_snr_5_weight will follow the same

_meta.explanation: The rule 'In RectifiedFlowLoss with weighting='min_snr_5', the loss weight reaches its maximum value of 5/6 when the SNR equals 5, which occurs at time t = 1/(1+√5).' does NOT hold here. The model assumes any time t plugged into min_snr_5_weight will follow the same SNR-to-weight mapping described in the rule. However, cosine_sample_median() returns t = 1 - cos(π/4) ≈ 0.2929, which yields SNR ≈ 8.24 > 5, so the min(snr, 5.0) clamps it to 5.0, but this t is NOT equal to 1/(1+√5) ≈ 0.3090, so the denominator (snr + 1) uses the unclamped SNR ≈ 8.24, not 5.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'In RectifiedFlowLoss with weighting='min_snr_5', the loss weight reaches its maximum value of 5/6 when the SNR equals 5, which occurs at time t = 1/(1+√5).' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
